hero.headline: JAIN Online MBA in Supply Chain, Production and Operations Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: JAIN (Deemed-to-be University) Online MBA with a Supply Chain, Production and Operations Management specialisation. NIRF #62 University, NAAC A++, UGC-DEB approved. Logistics and Supply Chain Management, Materials and Inventory Management, Agile Manufacturing and Lean Management, Export and Import Documentation, Information Systems for Logistics, and Sustainable Supply Chain in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: JAIN Online MBA Supply Chain, Production and Operations Management: NIRF #62, NAAC A++, fees Rs 1.75L-1.96L (3 payment options), 4 scholarship categories. Operations depth with Logistics and SCM, Materials and Inventory, Agile and Lean Manufacturing, Export-Import Documentation, Information Systems for Logistics, Negotiation Skills for SCM, Sustainable Supply Chain, and TQM.

about.body:
The JAIN Online MBA in Supply Chain, Production and Operations Management is offered by JAIN (Deemed-to-be University), Bengaluru. JAIN holds NAAC A++ accreditation, NIRF #62 University ranking (2025), and NIRF #73 in Management. The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The Supply Chain, Production and Operations Management specialisation delivers dedicated Sem 3-4 subjects covering Logistics and Supply Chain Management, Materials and Inventory Management, Agile Manufacturing and Lean Management, Export and Import Documentation, Information Systems for Logistics and SCM, Negotiation Skills for SCM Operations, Sustainable Supply Chain Management, and Total Quality Management.

Export and Import Documentation as a dedicated Sem 3 subject is uncommon among online MBA operations programmes - it directly targets logistics managers, customs coordinators, and international trade operations professionals. The combination of Lean and Agile Manufacturing, Sustainable Supply Chain, and SCM Negotiation Skills in Sem 3-4 gives this specialisation a comprehensive operations management toolkit applicable to manufacturing, e-commerce logistics, and international trade careers.

sem3Subjects.name: Business Research Methods
sem3Subjects.description: Apply quantitative research methods and data analysis to supply chain optimisation, logistics efficiency, and operations performance research.

sem3Subjects.name: Operations Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply capacity planning, process design, and operations strategy fundamentals as the foundation for supply chain specialisation subjects.

sem3Subjects.name: Logistics and Supply Chain Management
sem3Subjects.description: Design end-to-end supply chain networks, manage third-party logistics providers, and optimise warehousing and distribution systems.

sem3Subjects.name: Materials and Inventory Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply EOQ, JIT, and safety stock models to inventory optimisation, supplier management, and materials planning for manufacturing and retail.

sem3Subjects.name: Agile Manufacturing and Lean Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply lean production principles, 5S, Kaizen, value stream mapping, and agile manufacturing frameworks to factory and operations management.

sem3Subjects.name: Export and Import Documentation
sem3Subjects.description: Manage EXIM procedures, customs documentation, Letter of Credit, Bills of Lading, and regulatory compliance for international trade operations.

sem4Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem4Subjects.description: Develop supply chain-led competitive strategy, evaluate global sourcing decisions, and design operations management transformation roadmaps.

sem4Subjects.name: Information Systems for Logistics and SCM
sem4Subjects.description: Apply ERP systems, WMS platforms, TMS tools, and supply chain analytics dashboards to digital logistics and SCM operations management.

sem4Subjects.name: Negotiation Skills for SCM Operations
sem4Subjects.description: Negotiate vendor contracts, manage supplier relationship disputes, and apply structured negotiation frameworks to procurement and logistics deals.

sem4Subjects.name: Sustainable Supply Chain Management
sem4Subjects.description: Design green logistics strategies, apply ESG supply chain frameworks, and manage circular economy practices for sustainable operations roles.

sem4Subjects.name: Total Quality Management
sem4Subjects.description: Apply TQM frameworks, Six Sigma DMAIC methodology, ISO quality standards, and process control tools to manufacturing and service quality.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all JAIN MBA specialisations. See the main JAIN Online MBA page for core subject list.

whoHires.body:
Supply Chain, Production and Operations Management MBA graduates from JAIN Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Manufacturing, E-commerce and retail logistics, FMCG supply chain, Automotive and industrial, Pharmaceutical logistics, Export-import and international trade

**Entry-level roles:** Supply Chain Analyst, Logistics Executive, Materials Planner, Production Analyst, Import-Export Executive

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Supply Chain Manager, Logistics Manager, Procurement Manager, Operations Manager, Inventory Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Head of Supply Chain, VP Operations, Chief Supply Chain Officer, Director Logistics, VP Manufacturing

**Top hiring organisations:** Amazon Logistics India, Flipkart Supply Chain, Reliance Retail Logistics, D-Mart Operations, Mahindra Logistics, Blue Dart, Delhivery, DHL India, FedEx India, Maruti Suzuki Supply Chain, Tata Motors Operations, ITC Supply Chain, HUL Supply Chain, Cipla Pharma Logistics

skills.body:
The JAIN Supply Chain, Production and Operations Management specialisation builds skills that manufacturing, e-commerce logistics, and FMCG operations employers test at operations manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Logistics and Supply Chain Management: end-to-end supply chain network design, 3PL management, warehousing and distribution optimisation for SCM manager roles
- Materials and Inventory Management: EOQ and JIT inventory modelling, supplier management, and safety stock optimisation for procurement and inventory management
- Agile Manufacturing and Lean Management: lean production principles, value stream mapping, Kaizen, and 5S application for manufacturing and production management
- Information Systems for Logistics and SCM: ERP, WMS, and TMS platform management, supply chain analytics dashboards for digital logistics operations

**Applied skills:**
- Export and Import Documentation: EXIM procedures, customs documentation, Letter of Credit management, and international trade compliance for logistics and trade operations roles - a standalone subject uncommon in peer online MBA programmes
- Sustainable Supply Chain Management: ESG supply chain framework design, green logistics strategy, and circular economy operations for ESG-conscious manufacturing and FMCG companies

**Soft skills:**
- Negotiation Skills for SCM Operations: vendor contract negotiation, supplier dispute resolution, and procurement negotiation frameworks for operations and procurement management
- Communicating supply chain KPIs to CFO and operations leadership, managing cross-border logistics vendor relationships, and leading lean transformation initiatives across factory floors.

comparisons.body:
Comparing JAIN Online MBA Supply Chain, Production and Operations Management against peers:

**vs LPU Online MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management:** LPU Logistics and SCM at Rs 1,61,600-2,00,000 is NIRF #31. LPU covers logistics, SCM, and warehousing subjects. JAIN Supply Chain, Production and Operations Management adds Agile Manufacturing and Lean, Export-Import Documentation, Sustainable SCM, and Negotiation Skills for SCM as dedicated subjects. For manufacturing and lean production roles, JAIN's broader operations management curriculum is more comprehensive.

**vs Amity Online MBA Operations Management:** Amity Operations Management at Rs 2,25,000 covers operations strategy, process management, and supply chain with strong all-India brand recognition. JAIN's operations spec adds Export-Import Documentation, Negotiation Skills for SCM, and Sustainable Supply Chain as standalone subjects not typically in Amity's operations curriculum. For South India manufacturing and logistics careers, JAIN offers Bengaluru ecosystem access alongside these subject differentiators.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Operations and Data Sciences Management:** NMIMS Operations and Data Sciences combines quantitative analytics with operations management and carries strong Mumbai brand. For Mumbai manufacturing and pharma logistics, NMIMS carries stronger recognition. For Bengaluru and South India manufacturing, automotive, and e-commerce logistics careers, JAIN Supply Chain spec is well-positioned.

fees.body:
The JAIN Online MBA in Supply Chain, Production and Operations Management is priced between Rs 1,75,000 and Rs 1,96,000 total program fee across 4 semesters (2-year program). JAIN offers different fee tiers based on specialisation - contact our counsellor for exact fee for this specific specialisation.

**Payment options (3 ways)**:

- **Semester-wise**: Rs 43,750 per semester (Tier 1) or Rs 49,000 per semester (Tier 2)
- **Yearly**: Rs 87,500 per year (Tier 1) or Rs 98,000 per year (Tier 2)
- **One-time upfront**: Rs 1,75,000 (Tier 1) or Rs 1,96,000 (Tier 2)

**Additional charges (Indian students)**:
- Registration fee: Rs 2,500 one-time
- Examination fee: Rs 3,000 per year (Rs 6,000 total for 2 years)

**Total effective cost (Indian student)**:
- Tier 1 specs: Rs 1,83,500
- Tier 2 specs: Rs 2,04,500

**International student fees**:
- SAARC countries: additional Rs 11,250 registration + Rs 3,750 per year exam fee
- Other international students: USD 150 registration + USD 50 per year exam fee

**EMI options**: Available through JAIN's direct NBFC lending partner tie-ups (same pattern as LPU and MUJ - not through external marketing partners). Contact our counsellor for current EMI tenure options, approval terms, and whether zero-cost or interest-bearing.

**Scholarships available** (4 categories - specific discount % and eligibility varies by admission cycle):
- **Merit-based**: Academic excellence scholarship for qualifying marks
- **Armed Forces**: Support for serving/veteran military personnel and families
- **Gender-focused**: Empowerment scholarship for diverse applicants
- **Freshers**: Transition support for recent undergraduates

Specific discount percentages are confirmed at admission based on eligibility. Contact our counsellor for current cycle scholarship details.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with JAIN each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees, scholarship eligibility, and EMI terms with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by JAIN (Deemed-to-be University) and carries the JAIN name with the MBA designation. The Supply Chain, Production and Operations Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from JAIN Online MBA Supply Chain, Production and Operations Management alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Santosh Reddy
items.liked: Export and Import Documentation was directly applicable to my international trade operations role. Agile Manufacturing and Lean subject gave frameworks I applied to our production line improvement project. Sustainable SCM was forward-looking.
items.disliked: Information Systems for SCM could include more hands-on ERP system training beyond conceptual WMS and TMS coverage.
items.body: I had 5 years in FMCG supply chain and the JAIN operations MBA helped me move into a supply chain manager role. JAIN brand was recognised at Bengaluru and Chennai manufacturing companies. Negotiation Skills for SCM was a practical differentiator.

items.name: Rajan Krishnamurthy
items.liked: Lean and Agile Manufacturing combined with TQM gave comprehensive quality and efficiency management depth. Export-Import Documentation was unique and directly applicable.
items.disliked: Operations analytics and quantitative methods could be stronger alongside the qualitative frameworks.
items.body: Good programme for South India manufacturing and logistics careers. Chennai automotive and pharma logistics companies recognised the JAIN operations credential. For pan-India operations brand recognition, Amity or LPU may carry stronger reach.

items.name: Praveen Nair
items.liked: Sustainable Supply Chain Management subject was excellent - rare in operations MBA programmes at this fee range.
items.disliked: LMS quality for some elective operations subjects was inconsistent. Live faculty sessions were limited for Sem 4 subjects.
items.body: Good credential for South India manufacturing and logistics operations. Content quality was strong for the fee. For Mumbai or Delhi manufacturing brand recognition, LPU or Amity may carry stronger visibility.

faqs.question: How are JAIN fees different for different specializations?
faqs.answer: JAIN offers two pricing tiers across its MBA specialisations. Tier 1 is Rs 1,75,000 total (plus Rs 2,500 registration and Rs 6,000 examination fees). Tier 2 is Rs 1,96,000 total (same additional charges). The tier applicable to the Supply Chain, Production and Operations Management specialisation should be confirmed with our counsellor.

faqs.question: What scholarships does JAIN offer for Online MBA?
faqs.answer: JAIN offers 4 scholarship categories: Merit-based (academic excellence), Armed Forces (military personnel and families), Gender-focused (diversity empowerment), and Freshers (recent graduates). Specific discount percentages and eligibility conditions are confirmed at admission and vary by intake cycle. Contact our counsellor for current cycle details.

faqs.question: Does JAIN offer dual specialization in Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Yes. JAIN offers 7+ dual specialisation options. For operations professionals interested in analytics, the Finance and Business Analytics dual or HR and Business Analytics dual add data tools to business management credentials. Operations-specific dual specs are not available in JAIN's current portfolio.

faqs.question: Does the JAIN operations MBA cover Export and Import Documentation?
faqs.answer: Yes. Export and Import Documentation is a dedicated Sem 3 subject covering EXIM procedures, customs documentation, Letter of Credit, Bills of Lading, and regulatory compliance for international trade operations. This subject is uncommon as a standalone subject in peer online MBA operations programmes and directly applies to logistics managers, customs coordinators, and international trade operations roles.

faqs.question: Is JAIN Supply Chain MBA suitable for e-commerce logistics roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. Logistics and Supply Chain Management, Information Systems for Logistics and SCM, and Negotiation Skills for SCM Operations are directly applicable to e-commerce last-mile logistics, 3PL management, and fulfilment centre operations. JAIN's Bengaluru location gives strong access to South India's large e-commerce logistics ecosystem including Amazon, Flipkart, and Meesho operations teams.

chooseIf: You want a NAAC A++ MBA with Lean Manufacturing, Export-Import Documentation, Sustainable SCM, and SCM Negotiation Skills from a Bengaluru university.
You are targeting supply chain manager, operations manager, or logistics manager roles in South India manufacturing, FMCG, or e-commerce.
You want 3 flexible payment options and 4 scholarship categories at fees of Rs 1.75L-1.96L.
consider: If pan-India operations management brand recognition is the priority, LPU Online MBA Logistics and SCM or Amity Online MBA Operations offer stronger national recognition.
If analytics-integrated operations management is the goal, NMIMS Online MBA Operations and Data Sciences Management combines quantitative analytics with operations subjects.
If zero-cost EMI and North India manufacturing careers are the priority, LPU Online MBA Logistics and SCM at Rs 1,61,600 with grant and zero-cost EMI is a strong alternative.
